seoTitle: How to Integrate Airtable With SharePoint
metaDescription: Sync Airtable records to Microsoft SharePoint using Automations: set a trigger, authenticate your Microsoft account, and map fields to a SharePoint list.
intro: Bridging Airtable and SharePoint lets you keep your relational data in sync with Microsoft's document and list infrastructure without manual re-entry. This guide covers building an Airtable Automation that connects to SharePoint and syncs records automatically.

steps.heading: Set up status fields
steps.body: In your Airtable base, prepare a field to track which records are ready for syncing.

steps.heading: Open Automations
steps.body: Navigate to the Automations dashboard and create a new integration workflow.

steps.heading: Configure the trigger
steps.body: Set up a 'When record matches conditions' trigger to detect approved rows ready for synchronization.

steps.heading: Connect Microsoft SharePoint
steps.body: Add a new action step, select the Microsoft SharePoint connector, and authenticate your Microsoft account to grant access.

steps.heading: Map fields and test
steps.body: Choose whether to update a SharePoint List or generate document folders, map your table columns to matching SharePoint fields, and run a live test to confirm data transfers correctly.

faq.question: Do I need a Microsoft 365 account to connect SharePoint?
faq.answer: Yes, you'll need an active Microsoft account with appropriate SharePoint access permissions to authenticate the connection.

faq.question: Can the automation create new SharePoint items, not just update existing ones?
faq.answer: Yes, depending on how you configure the action step, the automation can create new SharePoint list rows or document folders based on Airtable records.

faq.question: How do I troubleshoot a failed sync?
faq.answer: Airtable's Automations panel keeps an execution history log where you can review failed runs and see what error occurred.

faq.question: Is this integration one-way or two-way?
faq.answer: Airtable Automations trigger actions in SharePoint based on changes in Airtable, making it primarily a one-way sync from Airtable to SharePoint.
